agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Lease of Coal Harbour Seawalk portion near 333 Menchions Mews
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider leasing an 11.8 sq m portion of the Coal Harbour Seawalk to 562270 B.C. Ltd. (owner of 333 Menchions Mews) for an enclosed lobby for Lift Bar & Grill. The proposed lease is for 10 years plus one 10-year renewal option, at $1,500 annual rent inclusive of property taxes. The lease would be retroactive to December 1, 2024, and includes a 12-month cancellation clause for municipal use.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on May 19, 2026 — this could affect public access to the seawalk.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Coal Harbour Seawalk lease

agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	Lease of a Portion of the Coal Harbour Seawalk, Adjacent to 333 Menchions Mews
Report

attachments.title: 1.	Lease of a Portion of the Coal Harbour Seawalk, Adjacent to 333 Menchions Mews — Report

agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Sign bylaw amendment for PNE Exhibition Park at 2901 East Hastings Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on May 19, 2026 on a proposed amendment to the Sign By-law to create a new Sign District for PNE Exhibition Park at 2901 East Hastings Street. If approved, the amendment would permit up to three new electronic static image signs with first-party and third-party advertising, and would also amend the Sign Fee By-law. The new district applies only to the PNE property and does not include city street rights-of-way.

key_dates.label: Council vote on PNE sign bylaw amendment

agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	Sign By-law Amendment - PNE Exhibition Park (2901 East Hastings Street)
Report

attachments.title: 2.	Sign By-law Amendment - PNE Exhibition Park (2901 East Hastings Street) — Report

agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Capital grant for social housing at 1395 Rolston Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a capital grant of up to $30 million to More Than a Roof Mennonite Housing Society for construction of approximately 212 social housing units (52% family units) with retail at grade on City-owned land at 1395 Rolston Street. Approval requires at least 8 affirmative votes (two-thirds of Council) under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter. The grant will be disbursed once all permits and funding are in place, prior to construction start.

key_dates.label: Council vote on $30M social housing grant at 1395 Rolston Street

agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	Capital Grant to More Than a Roof Housing Society for Development of Social Housing at 1395 Rolston Street
Recommendation A requires at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report

attachments.title: 3.	Capital Grant to More Than a Roof Housing Society for Development of Social Housing at 1395 Rolston Street — Report

agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to vote on large electronic advertising signs on streets
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the Street and Traffic By-law that would allow large-format electronic advertising signs on city streets. If approved, this would permit digital billboards and similar electronic signs in certain locations. The proposal is still under consideration and has not been approved yet. Business owners who use or are affected by street signage should be aware of this potential change.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your signage options or advertising costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.	Amendments to the Street and Traffic By-law to Authorize Large-Format Electronic Advertising Signs on Streets
Report
(Revised on May 13, 2026)

attachments.title: 4.	Amendments to the Street and Traffic By-law to Authorize Large-Format Electronic Advertising Signs on Streets — Report

agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on modifying conditions for West Georgia, Burrard, Alberni development
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on May 19, 2026 on a staff recommendation to extend the deadline for enacting the CD-1 By-law for four rezoning applications at 1450 West Georgia Street, 1157 Burrard Street, 1640-1650 Alberni Street, and 1668-1684 Alberni Street to May 31, 2028. The extension relates to conditions for a Community Amenity Contribution (CAC). If approved, the change would give developers an additional two years to finalize the bylaw enactment, potentially delaying construction timelines and related impacts on nearby businesses.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit comments to Vancouver City Council before the May 19, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Council vote on CD-1 bylaw deadline extension

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning for 717-743 West 28th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on a proposed CD-1 rezoning for 717-743 West 28th Avenue. The application seeks to rezone from RM-8A to CD-1, increasing maximum floor space ratio from 1.2 to 3.0 and maximum building height from 11.5 m to 21 m, to permit a six-storey, 100% market rental building with 94 units. The site is within 700 m of King Edward SkyTrain Station and is being assessed under the Transit-Oriented Areas Rezoning Policy.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review the draft CD-1 bylaw (Appendix A) and conditions of approval (Appendix B) linked in the agenda; submit comments to Council before the May 19, 2026 meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning for 2244–2280 West 6th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a bylaw to rezone 2244–2280 West 6th Avenue from R5-4 to CD-1, allowing two 22-storey towers (84.0 m max height) with 416 rental units. A minimum of 20% of total dwelling unit area must be below-market rental units. At least 35% of below-market and other units must be family-sized (2+ bedrooms), with at least 25% of all units as 2-bedroom and 10% as 3-bedroom. Maximum floor space ratio is 6.5 on a site area of 4,179.9 m². No public hearing will be held as the proposal is consistent with the Vancouver Official Development Plan and Broadway Plan.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the May 19, 2026 council meeting and consider submitting comments to Council before that date if this rezoning affects your business or property.

agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a proposed rezoning of four parcels at 4339-4387 Cambie Street and 506 West 27th Avenue from R1-1 to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) District. The plan permits a nine-storey mixed-use building with 148 strata housing units and ground-floor commercial space, increasing maximum floor space ratio from 0.7 to 3.6 and maximum building height from 11.5 m (38 ft) to 28.7 m (94 ft). The public hearing is scheduled for May 19, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning for 2120-2130 West 10th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on a proposed rezoning of 2120-2130 West 10th Avenue from C-8 (Commercial) to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) to allow a 20-storey, 120-unit rental building with 20% below-market units and ground-floor commercial space. The maximum floor space ratio would increase from 2.25 to 7.7, and maximum height from 12.2 m to 70 m. At least 35% of below-market and other units must be family housing (2+ bedrooms), with a minimum of 25% two-bedroom and 10% three-bedroom units. A minimum of 300 m² of commercial floor area is required.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to vote on fast-tracking rental housing updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote May 19, 2026 on bylaw amendments to add a new RR-3C residential rental district, repeal the RR-2C district, and rezone four properties: 4708-4742 Joyce St & 3604 Tanner St to RR-2B; 4471-4485 Fraser St & 691 E 29th Ave to RR-3C; 2005-2045 W 49th Ave to RR-2B; and 1401-1455 E 49th Ave to RR-3C. The changes also update parking, sign, noise, and zoning bylaws to include RR-3C and remove RR-2C. In RR-2B, maximum floor space ratio increases to 3.00 if 20% of floor area is below-market rental or 100% is social housing, and building height increases to 6 storeys (8 in transit-oriented areas). The 20% below-market requirement for RR-2C is removed.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on May 19, 2026 — if approved, these bylaw changes will affect development options on the listed properties and for RR district projects city-wide.

key_dates.label: Council vote on RR-3C district, RR-2C repeal, and rezoning applications

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Text amendment for CD-1 zoning at 133-159 West 49th Avenue, Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a bylaw amendment to increase the maximum floor space ratio (FSR) from 2.5 to 2.62 for the CD-1 zoned property at 133-159 West 49th Avenue. The change would allow mezzanines within existing double-height spaces in ground-floor commercial retail units. No other changes to the building are proposed. The application was referred to Council on May 5, 2026, and is up for a vote on May 19, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on May 19, 2026 — this zoning change could affect development plans for these properties.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to fast-track rental housing and small hotels in new mixed-use district
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has enacted By-law amendments creating a new C-2A mixed-use commercial district that fast-tracks approvals for rental housing and small hotels. The new district allows up to 6 storeys and 3.5 FSR (3.7 FSR on large corner sites). Rental projects seeking density above 2.5 FSR and height above 4 storeys must provide 35% family units (2+ bedrooms). Rezoning for rental housing in C-2A will only be considered in exceptional circumstances; most projects must proceed through the district schedule. The amendments take effect May 19, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review whether your development site falls within the new C-2A district and confirm your project meets the as-of-right height, density, and family-unit requirements before applying.

key_dates.label: C-2A district bylaw amendments take effect

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver bylaw to authorize electronic signs on streets
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the Street and Traffic By-law to allow electronic signs on city streets. If approved, this would permit businesses and others to use digital or electronic signs in public street spaces, subject to the rules in the new bylaw. The decision is not final yet — it is up for approval at a future council meeting, pending the approval of a related report.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect your business's ability to install electronic signs on streets.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Amend Downtown Eastside/Oppenheimer plan for new C-2A district
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to amend the Downtown Eastside/Oppenheimer Official Development Plan (the long-term blueprint for land use in that area) to create a new 'C-2A' commercial district. This proposed change would update the rules for what kinds of businesses and buildings are allowed in that part of the Downtown Eastside. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this could affect property use and development options in the Downtown Eastside.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body: 13.	A By-law to amend Downtown Eastside/Oppenheimer Official Development Plan By-law No. 5532 regarding the new C-2A district
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New parking rules for Vancouver's C-2A district
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a bylaw that would change parking requirements for new developments in the newly created C-2A zoning district. If approved, this could affect how many parking spots businesses and new buildings in this area must provide. The proposed bylaw amends the existing Parking By-law No. 6059 to add rules specific to the C-2A district. This is not yet law — it is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, these new parking rules will apply to any new development or business in the C-2A district.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body: 12.	A By-law to amend Parking By-law No. 6059 regarding the new C-2A district
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver noise bylaw changes for new C-2A district
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend Vancouver's Noise Control By-law No. 6555. The change would set new noise rules for a brand new zoning district called C-2A. If approved, this would affect what noise levels are allowed in that area, which could impact businesses like restaurants, bars, and construction projects. The decision has not been made yet — this is still a proposal up for discussion.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if passed, check whether your business is in the new C-2A zone and review the new noise limits.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.	A By-law to amend Noise Control By-law No. 6555 regarding the new C-2A district
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver sign bylaw changes for new C-2A district
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend Vancouver's Sign By-law No.11879. The change would create new sign rules for a brand-new zoning district called C-2A. If approved, businesses in this new district would need to follow different rules for their signs. The exact details of the new sign rules are not yet public. This is a proposal — it has not been passed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, check if your property is in the new C-2A district and review the new sign rules.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver to amend subdivision rules for new C-2A zone
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend Vancouver's Subdivision By-law No. 5208. The change would add rules for a new land-use zone called 'C-2A'. If approved, this would affect how property owners can subdivide (legally split) land in that zone. The exact details of the C-2A zone and the new subdivision rules are not yet public. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved or enacted yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and public hearing notice — this could affect how you can subdivide your property in the new C-2A zone.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.	A By-law to amend Subdivision By-law No. 5208 regarding new C-2A district
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver bylaw change to protect rental housing in new C-2A zone
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend the Rental Housing Stock Official Development Plan (ODP) Bylaw No. 9488. This change would apply to the new C-2A district. In plain language, the ODP is the city's long-term plan for rental housing. The proposed amendment would update the rules for this new commercial-residential zone to ensure rental housing is protected or replaced when redevelopment happens. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments if you own rental property in a C-2A zone.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.	A By-law to amend the Rental Housing Stock Official Development Plan By-law No. 9488 regarding the new C-2A district
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New C-2A zoning district and bylaw amendments in Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a new C-2A zoning district and make other minor changes to the city's Zoning and Development By-law. This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet. If passed, the new C-2A district could affect where certain types of businesses, such as retail or commercial operations, are allowed to open or operate. The proposal is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments if your property or business could be affected by a new zoning district.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for 282 West 49th Avenue social housing
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a housing agreement for a social housing project at 282 West 49th Avenue. A housing agreement is a legal contract between the city and the property owner that locks in the building's use as affordable rental housing for a set number of years. This proposal has not been approved yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 6.	A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 282 West 49th Avenue (Social Housing)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for 282 West 49th Avenue (secured market rental)
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a Housing Agreement for the property at 282 West 49th Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in certain conditions — in this case, the agreement would require the rental units in this building to stay as secured market rental housing. This means the owner cannot convert the building to condos or other uses for the life of the agreement. Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting. It has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 5.	A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 282 West 49th Avenue (Secured Market Rental)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to repeal housing agreement for 1289 Nicola Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would repeal an existing Housing Agreement for the property at 1289 Nicola Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in affordable housing requirements, like rent limits or income restrictions, for a set number of years. If this bylaw passes, those affordability rules would be removed from this specific property. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if passed, this could affect affordable housing protections at this address.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 4.	A By-law to repeal By-law No. 13307 authorizing a Housing Agreement for 1289 Nicola Street
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver to amend utility development cost levy bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would make miscellaneous amendments to the Vancouver Utilities Development Cost Levy By-law. This by-law sets fees that developers must pay to help fund new water, sewer, and drainage infrastructure. If approved, the changes could affect the costs of new construction and development projects in Vancouver. The exact nature of the amendments is not detailed in the agenda item, but any change to these levies can impact the overall cost of building new homes, commercial spaces, or other developments.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your development project costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — May 19, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver development cost levy bylaw amendments
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a bylaw that would make miscellaneous amendments to Vancouver's Development Cost Levy By-law No. 9755. A development cost levy is a fee the city charges developers to help pay for new infrastructure like parks, water pipes, and roads that are needed because of new buildings. If approved, these changes would update how the levy is calculated or applied. This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, these changes could affect the fees you pay on new development projects.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone 4910-4950 Willow Street to CD-1 in Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 4910-4950 Willow Street to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning district created for a specific development, rather than following standard city-wide rules. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet. Councillors Bligh, Fry, Maloney, and Orr must review the proceedings before they are eligible to vote on this matter.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own property or have a business interest near 4910-4950 Willow Street, watch for the council vote and consider attending the public hearing to have your say.

agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will consider a referral report on May 19, 2026 to rezone 1500-1580 West 3rd Avenue from IC-2 (Industrial) to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development).
